commit.message:
fix: add GitHub Pages origin to default CORS origins

The CORS_ORIGINS env var was not set on Render, so the default
only included localhost. Add the GitHub Pages domain as a default.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix backend crash: replace sys.exit(1) with exception when config.ini missing

web_app/app.py called sys.exit(1) at module level when config.ini was
not found. SystemExit bypasses the except Exception handler in jobs.py,
killing the gunicorn worker in a crash loop. Replace with RuntimeError
so the import fails gracefully and the API runs without Google Sheets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add dbg_macro lint to workspace-wide clippy config

Add clippy::dbg_macro = 'warn' to workspace lints in Cargo.toml.
This prevents accidental dbg!() calls from shipping in production
code. Test code is already allowed via CI clippy relaxation
(-A clippy::dbg_macro in quick.sh).

No violations exist in the codebase — this is a preventive lint.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: speed up cache refresh with parallel regions, bulk pricing, and batch writes

Three optimizations to reduce cache refresh time from 20-30min to ~2min:

1. Parallel regions (ThreadPoolExecutor max_workers=10)
   - All 36 regions now refresh concurrently instead of serially
   - ~10x wall-clock time reduction

2. Bulk pricing fetch (fetch_bulk_region_pricing)
   - Single paginated API call per region to prices.azure.com
   - ~20x fewer HTTP requests vs per-SKU fetching
   - Preserves Linux/Windows price selection and fallback logic

3. Batch Table Storage writes (submit_transaction in groups of 100)
   - Azure Table Storage transaction limit is 100 ops per batch
   - ~100x fewer round-trips to storage per region
   - Falls back to individual upserts if a batch fails

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add workflow arrows between command bar tabs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add `wave:term` component with direct SSE output + `/api/terminput` input path (#2974)

This PR introduces a standalone Tsunami terminal element (`wave:term`)
and routes terminal IO outside the normal render/event loop for
lower-latency streaming. It adds imperative terminal output
(`TermWrite`) over SSE and terminal input/resize delivery over a
dedicated `/api/terminput` endpoint.

- **Frontend: new `wave:term` element**
  - Added `tsunami/frontend/src/element/tsunamiterm.tsx`.
  - Uses `@xterm/xterm` with `@xterm/addon-fit`.
- Renders as an outer `<div>` (style/class/ref target), with xterm
auto-fit to that container.
  - Supports ref passthrough on the outer element.

- **Frontend: terminal transport wiring**
  - Registered `wave:term` in `tsunami/frontend/src/vdom.tsx`.
- Added SSE listener handling for `termwrite` in
`tsunami/frontend/src/model/tsunami-model.tsx`, dispatched to the
terminal component via a local custom event.
- `onData` and `onResize` now POST directly to `/api/terminput` as JSON
payloads:
    - `id`
    - `data64` (base64 terminal input)
    - `termsize` (`rows`, `cols`) for resize updates

- **Backend: new terminal IO APIs**
- Added `/api/terminput` handler in `tsunami/engine/serverhandlers.go`.
  - Added protocol types in `tsunami/rpctypes/protocoltypes.go`:
    - `TermInputPacket`, `TermWritePacket`, `TermSize`
  - Added engine/client support in `tsunami/engine/clientimpl.go`:
    - `SendTermWrite(id, data64)` -> emits SSE event `termwrite`
    - `SetTermInputHandler(...)` and `HandleTermInput(...)`
  - Exposed app-level APIs in `tsunami/app/defaultclient.go`:
    - `TermWrite(id, data64) error`
    - `SetTermInputHandler(func(TermInputPacket))`

- **Example usage**
  ```go
  app.SetTermInputHandler(func(input app.TermInputPacket) {
      // input.Id, input.Data64, input.TermSize.Rows/Cols
  })

  _ = app.TermWrite("term1", "SGVsbG8gZnJvbSB0aGUgYmFja2VuZA0K")
  ```

- **<screenshot>**
- Provided screenshot URL:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/58c92ebb-0a52-43d2-b577-17c9cf92a19c

numerology master numbers, and Fibonacci phi resonance.

Add EphemerisCacheService for pre-computed planetary positions

Pre-computes planetary positions for 20 years (2016-2036) at hourly
resolution using SwissEphNet, eliminating per-call computation during
backtesting. Uses ConcurrentDictionary for thread-safe reads and
SemaphoreSlim to prevent duplicate warm-up runs.

Fix Helm workflow: update package path to helm/aws-route53-zone-exporter after charts dir rename

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Teamwork 0305 2 (#100)

This pull request introduces several improvements across the server and
web client, focusing on robust server error handling, accessibility
enhancements, UI polish, and expanded model configuration defaults. The
most significant changes include better handling of server port
conflicts and runtime errors, ensuring all built-in roles have default
model configurations, and improving accessibility by adding ARIA labels
and button types. Additionally, UI logic has been refined to prevent
duplicate chat messages, and the web client now applies the correct
theme before rendering to avoid flashes of incorrect styles.

**Server robustness and error handling:**

* Added a permanent error handler to the HTTP server in `index.ts` to
catch and log unexpected runtime errors (such as `EACCES` and
`ECONNRESET`), then exit cleanly. Startup now also provides clearer
messaging when all ports are in use, including a suggested command to
kill existing processes. (`packages/server/src/index.ts`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-8e6dfbbfb522575fe7c568c2de518bf9de351de83c9dbb48eaa865cd54450eb3R369-R376)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-8e6dfbbfb522575fe7c568c2de518bf9de351de83c9dbb48eaa865cd54450eb3R393-R395)
* The WebSocket server now logs errors instead of crashing the process,
catching both startup and runtime errors.
(`packages/server/src/comms/WebSocketServer.ts`)
* Added comprehensive tests for server error handling, including port
conflict resolution and error event handling for both HTTP and WebSocket
servers. (`packages/server/src/__tests__/ServerErrorHandling.test.ts`)

**Model configuration defaults:**

* Expanded `DEFAULT_MODEL_CONFIG` to include defaults for all 14
built-in roles, ensuring every role has an associated model list. Added
corresponding tests to verify coverage and correctness.
(`packages/server/src/projects/ModelConfigDefaults.ts`,
`packages/server/src/__tests__/ModelConfig.test.ts`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-63fbbbd3e798727b00f9b4d497870bffa30eebf3ff9aa69bf3429dc943647c97R45-R51)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-8ffb08338cd7d80a654cf827604c9855db0895f79c3fc2fbcff6be99c3a0c152R125-R166)

**Accessibility and UI improvements:**

* Added `type="button"` and appropriate `aria-label` attributes to
interactive buttons across multiple components to improve accessibility
and prevent accidental form submissions.
(`packages/web/src/components/DataBrowser/DataBrowser.tsx`,
`packages/web/src/components/FolderPicker/FolderPicker.tsx`,
`packages/web/src/components/LeadDashboard/LeadDashboard.tsx`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-bd03bdf9f6b3a4bfcf96093d22931c9deed5f3c2290dedc3e393cdb29e4f4933L48-R48)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-6cc3e8b47f6f9ed2e77d21b5d10f657cb4f7e72bff119317cb01183ebb0db6edL64-R73)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-6ae88e7bcc2ac8fe8ff839409f271810783df7956c7cf86a959bb26296e1198dL1668-R1667)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-6ae88e7bcc2ac8fe8ff839409f271810783df7956c7cf86a959bb26296e1198dR1753-R1754)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-6ae88e7bcc2ac8fe8ff839409f271810783df7956c7cf86a959bb26296e1198dR1777-R1778)
[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-6ae88e7bcc2ac8fe8ff839409f271810783df7956c7cf86a959bb26296e1198dL1936-R1930)

**Chat and message UI logic:**

* Updated the lead dashboard logic to prevent duplicate display of
broadcast, inter-agent DM, and group messages in the chat panel; these
are now shown only in the comms panel or groups tab.
(`packages/web/src/components/LeadDashboard/LeadDashboard.tsx`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-6ae88e7bcc2ac8fe8ff839409f271810783df7956c7cf86a959bb26296e1198dL521-R521)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-6ae88e7bcc2ac8fe8ff839409f271810783df7956c7cf86a959bb26296e1198dL535-R533)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-6ae88e7bcc2ac8fe8ff839409f271810783df7956c7cf86a959bb26296e1198dL565-R559)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-6ae88e7bcc2ac8fe8ff839409f271810783df7956c7cf86a959bb26296e1198dR1554-R1559)

**Web client theming:**

* Improved theme handling by injecting a script (`theme-init.js`) to set
the theme class on the HTML element before paint, preventing a flash of
the wrong theme. Updated `index.html` to reference this script and the
favicon. (`packages/web/index.html`,
`packages/web/public/theme-init.js`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5c0e1d6a0de94ea1723b573b1e0ed0100423b33a3fcefea81d8bfbc67b113010L2-R8)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-7446f04682d630a6071eff1b3aeee55be8e222679adafd6e74b2abcfa5c58643R1-R13)

---------

commit.message:
feat(hid-transport): implement macOS IOKit HID driver abstraction (#192)

Add IOKit-based HID transport for macOS device communication:
- IOHIDManager device enumeration with VID/PID filtering
- IOHIDDevice open/close/read/write operations
- Hot-plug detection via IOKit callbacks
- Safe Rust wrappers around IOKit FFI
- Comprehensive tests with mock IOKit backend

The implementation is structured as:
- macos/mod.rs: Platform-agnostic data structures (matching dicts, elements,
  descriptors, report parsing, error types) - compiles on all platforms
- macos/iokit_ffi.rs: IOKit/CoreFoundation FFI bindings with safe RAII
  wrappers (cfg(target_os = macos) only)
- macos/device.rs: MacOSDeviceHandle with state machine, pre-allocated
  report buffers, and FFI device operations
- macos/enumeration.rs: MacOSHidEnumerator with device filtering,
  add/remove diffing, and FFI system enumeration
- macos/hotplug.rs: HotplugEvent types, event collector, and
  CFRunLoop-based IOKit monitor

All 42 new tests run on Windows/Linux using platform-agnostic mock data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(engine): add deep blackbox, safety, and pipeline determinism tests (#191)

Add 96 new tests across 3 focus areas for 1.0 RC quality validation:

Blackbox Recording (30 tests):
- Recording capacity and mixed-stream behavior
- Stream A/B/C serialization and rate limiting
- RT timing pressure (frame recording latency)
- Export/import roundtrip for header, footer, index entries
- Index creation via finalization for large recordings
- Data integrity: crash simulation, CRC determinism
- Compression levels (0 and 9), disabled streams

Safety State Machine (38 tests):
- All valid state transitions (SafeTorque→Challenge→Awaiting→HighTorque)
- Invalid transition rejection (wrong token, wrong state, etc.)
- Fault escalation timing (<10ms detection, <50ms response)
- Multi-fault concurrent scenarios (sequential, during challenge)
- Recovery paths from every state
- Interlock challenge-response (expiry, combo hold, consent)
- Proptest: random fault sequences preserve safety invariants

FFB Pipeline Determinism (28 tests):
- Identical inputs produce bit-identical outputs
- Bounded execution time (empty and compiled pipelines)
- Filter chain composition (torque cap, passthrough, swap)
- Torque output always within safe bounds [-1, 1]
- Validation: non-monotonic curves, invalid reconstruction rejected
- Response curve integration and hash determinism
- Proptest: random inputs never produce NaN or out-of-range torque

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Revert "Revert 'Increase number of assertions (GlobalAP) + VN cache (#124132)'" (#125215)

[Diffs](https://dev.azure.com/dnceng-public/public/_build/results?buildId=1321789&view=ms.vss-build-web.run-extensions-tab)

Re-applies [#124132](https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/pull/124132)
which was reverted in
[#124928](https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/pull/124928) due to
excessive JIT memory allocation. That root cause has since been fixed.

## Description

**`assertionprop.cpp` / `compiler.h` — VN existence cache**
- Adds `optAssertionVNsMap` (`VNSet*`): lazily-initialized map tracking
which VNs have at least one assertion
- Adds `optAssertionHasAssertionsForVN(vn, addIfNotFound)`: O(1) lookup;
when `addIfNotFound=true` registers the VN on first insertion
- `optAddAssertion`: uses the cache to gate the full O(n) table scan for
duplicates; also registers `op2` bound VN and the `VN + const` operand
VN (fix from [#124306](https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/pull/124306))
so `MergeEdgeAssertions` can find them
- `optAssertionProp_LclVar`: early-outs via cache before iterating
assertions; pre-computes `treeVN` once instead of re-deriving it in the
loop
- `optAssertionVNIsNonNull`: skips iteration when cache says no
assertions exist for the VN

**`assertionprop.cpp` — `optMaxAssertionCount` heuristic**
- Replaces the IL-code-size step-function table with the
empirically-validated formula:
  ```
  max(64, min(256, (lvaTrackedCount + 3 * fgBBcount + 48) >> 2))
  ```
Validated across 1.1M compiled methods: 94.6% stay at the 64 floor, 0.4%
hit the 256 cap, worst-case underprediction of 127 for 481 methods
(0.043%).

**`rangecheck.cpp`**
- `MergeEdgeAssertions`: replaces `normalLclVN == NoVN` guard with
`!optAssertionHasAssertionsForVN(normalLclVN)` — handles the `NoVN` case
internally and also skips the loop when no relevant assertions were ever
registered.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

✨ Let Copilot coding agent [set things up for
you](https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/new?title=✨+Set+up+Copilot+instructions&body=Configure%20instructions%20for%20this%20repository%20as%20documented%20in%20%5BBest%20practices%20for%20Copilot%20coding%20agent%20in%20your%20repository%5D%28https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips%29%2E%0A%0A%3COnboard%20this%20repo%3E&assignees=copilot)
— coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for
your repo.

commit.message:
Fix Hurst division by zero and ProphecyEngine TopHeadlines null safety

- Add guard for logReturns.Count <= 2 in CalculateHurstExponent to
  prevent Math.Log(1) = 0 division by zero
- Replace .First() with .FirstOrDefault() and add null check for
  TopHeadlines in ProphecyEngine sentiment integration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: deny unwrap/expect in production code for all publishable crates

Add #![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used))]
to 23 crates that were missing the safety lint. This ensures that any future
unwrap() or expect() call in production code will be caught at compile time.

The cfg_attr(not(test)) guard allows tests to continue using unwrap/expect
freely, matching the pattern already used in copybook-codec, copybook-core,
copybook-fixed, copybook-rdw, copybook-record-io, copybook-overpunch, and
other established crates.

Affected crates:
- copybook-arrow, copybook-cli-determinism, copybook-codec-memory
- copybook-contracts, copybook-corruption, copybook-corruption-detectors
- copybook-corruption-predicates, copybook-corruption-rdw, copybook-dialect
- copybook-error-reporter, copybook-lexer, copybook-options
- copybook-overflow, copybook-rdw-predicates, copybook-safe-index
- copybook-safe-ops, copybook-safe-text, copybook-support-matrix
- copybook-utils, copybook-zoned-format, copybook-error
- copybook-charset, copybook-codepage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: remove RenderableDataObject marker class (fixes #920)

- Delete unused DesignSession.cs (zero references)
- Change all 13 derivatives to inherit from BaseDataObject directly
- Remove convention-based field discovery (useConvention check) from DataScaffold
- Remove the empty RenderableDataObject marker class from DataObject.cs
- Add [DataEntity] and [DataField] attributes to test classes that relied on convention
- Update 7 documentation files to reference BaseDataObject

All entity types already had explicit [DataEntity] and [DataField] attributes,
so the convention fallback was redundant. Serialization via BinaryObjectSerializer
is unaffected — it discovers properties via reflection independently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(email-productivity-agent): apply 20 MUST-FIX findings from AI Council Round 2

Round 2 review (4 sessions, 12 expert personas) verified all 17 Round 1
fixes and found 20 new MUST-FIX items. All 20 addressed:

Data layer (5 fixes):
- R2A-01: Reduce cr_recipientemail 254->250 to keep composite key at 900 bytes
- R2A-02: Fix cr_originalmessageid MaxLength in script (200->500) to match schema
- R2A-03: Fix primaryColumn in snoozed schema to cr_originalsubject (matches script)
- R2A-04: Fix WholeNumber min/max (0-365->1-30), add server-side defaults (3,5,1,7)
- R2A-05: Add missing cr_owneruserid to default NudgeConfig creation in flow doc

Flow logic (6 fixes):
- R2B-01: Fix sentDateTime vs receivedDateTime mismatch in Flow 1 upsert
- R2B-02: Fix THREAD_EXCERPT to reference Compose output (not raw HTTP)
- R2B-03: Add Select action before join() to extract bodyPreview from objects
- R2B-04: Add missing Get_my_profile_(V2) Step 0 to Flow 3
- R2B-05: Fix Flow 1 action name Get_my_profile -> Get_my_profile_(V2)
- R2B-06: Add missing Get_my_profile_(V2) Step 1b to Flow 2

Deployment guide (9 fixes):
- R2D-01: Fix Step 9 snooze variable names (SNOOZED_SUBJECT, NEW_MESSAGE_SENDER)
- R2D-02: Rewrite Step 8 to avoid duplicate environment creation
- R2D-03: Add SnoozedConversation to Step 1 output list
- R2D-04: Replace ambiguous 'System topic' with Settings -> Agent inputs
- R2D-05: Change DAYS_SINCE_SENT from 'Business days' to 'Calendar days'
- R2D-06: Add  auth preamble to GDPR erasure script
- R2D-07: Add DLP policy check to prerequisites
- R2D-08: Rewrite Step 11 for connector-based auth model
- R2D-09: Add HTTP with Azure AD connection config values

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
